day_summary.overall: Sasol moved from Initial Interest to Qualified, but the flurry of health-check flags logged the same day tells the real story: this is a paper advance, not a momentum signal. The account has gone quiet (no inbound in 14+ days), there's no confirmed decision maker, and the org chart/multi-threading work hasn't been done — so the qualification is provisional until basic account intelligence catches up.

best_practice_check.summary: The deal's core deficit is access to power and multi-threading, not competitive positioning; both need direct, deliberate action before any RFP motion can realistically restart.

competitive_position.summary: Competing against SAP Ariba (primary) and Taulia (secondary) in a stalled RFI/RFP cycle that has gone dark for 173 days. Ariba is the entrenched threat given Sasol's likely SAP ERP footprint; Taulia targets working-capital optimization. Zycus has not positioned a differentiated wedge or secured a champion to navigate the competitive dynamics. The path forward is to restart engagement with a head-to-head AI/ML capability comparison (Zycus iAnalyze + iRisk vs Ariba's static analytics) anchored to a quantified pain, delivered via an executive briefing or design workshop.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: This is an early-stage, single-threaded RFI relationship that stalled after a one-hour demo eleven months ago and has had no verified buyer response of any kind in the last six months; nothing in the evidence supports treating this as an actively moving deal.

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded: the only consistently responsive contact is a mid-level commercial specialist (Estee Larkins); no multi-threading has been confirmed since the Aug 2025 demo invite list.
No compelling event or cost-of-inaction has ever been articulated by Sasol, and the close date has been treated as a placeholder rather than a buyer-driven date, currently sitting at 26 Feb 2027.

win_position.text: No economic buyer or champion has been identified in the twelve months since this opportunity opened (Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c=false, Decision_Maker_Identified__c=false), and the sole responsive contact, Estee Larkins, has behaved as a guarded gatekeeper, not an advocate (email, 18 Aug 2025).

Sasol's promised RFP, targeted for early-to-mid September 2025 following the RFI, has never been issued, and as of the March 2026 next-step note the RFI 'has not gained much traction' (Next_Step_History__c).

Competing against an embedded incumbent: Sasol runs this very RFI on the SAP Ariba portal it already uses, plus names Taulia as part of its current-state tooling (How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c).

deal_momentum.text: No verified buyer engagement has occurred since an inbound reply from Estee Larkins on 18 Aug 2025 (325 days ago); the last Salesforce activity of any kind was rep-initiated LinkedIn/email outreach on 15 Jan 2026 (175 days ago) that drew no reply.
The deal has sat in Qualified stage for roughly a year against a typical cadence of weeks, with no stage, forecast, or amount movement recorded in that time.

customer_commitment.text: Sasol has made no forward commitment since confirming the 20 Aug 2025 demo would be capped at one hour and used for its own questions (email, 18 Aug 2025); it has not confirmed attendance at the EY roundtable floated for Sasol in Oct/Nov 2025, nor responded to the Jan 2026 outreach or the offered design workshop (Mar 2026 note).

ai_positioning_strength.summary: No AI-specific conversation has been evidenced with the buyer; the AI intent signal itself reads negative (AIS_Status__c='Negative', 0 calls in the Avoma window), and outreach has leaned on generic AI/ANA messaging rather than a confirmed buyer AI need.
